bidding.note: 5-5 minors: open 1D (higher), rebid 2C shows the second five-card suit; clean 2C partscore making +2.
Standard 5d-4c mirror: open 1D (longest), rebid the cheap 2C; natural 2C making exactly.
Standard 5d-4c: 1D then 2C; 15 HCP top of range, 2C making +3, clean single teaching point.
5c-4d: open 1D (not 1C) so 2C rebid stays cheap; the core lesson point, 2C making +2.
Standard 5d-4c: 1D then 2C correct; wrinkle is the 2C partscore going down one on a light dummy.
5d-4c: 1D then 2C; North raised to invitational 3C and South correctly passed with a 12-count minimum, 3C +1.
5c-4d: open 1D not 1C to keep 2C cheap; 11 HCP minimum, natural 2C making exactly.
1D then 2C correct, but South's 4S raise over invitational 3S on a bare 11 and J8 doubleton is aggressive and buries the lesson in a spade-fit game (22 HCP, 4S just makes); Pass is the sober call.
Edge 5c-4d with a 4-card spade: after 1D-1H South's 1S shows the major (the debatable choice); rebidding 2C to show the longer club suit is equally defensible.
Balanced 13: open the longer minor 1C, rebid 1NT to show 12-14 balanced; textbook, 1NT down one is just card luck.
5-5 minors: 1D then 2C shows both suits; then a natural game auction to 3N (24 HCP) making exactly - sound but the auction runs past the partscore lesson.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C correct; North gives preference to 2D and it drifts down one - normal partscore wrinkle.

Opening 1D is right, but North's game-forcing 2C response bids South's second suit, so South never rebids 2C - the lesson's rebid never surfaces (South jumps to 3N instead). Sound bridge, poor example.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C; 15 HCP top of range, clean natural 2C partscore making exactly.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C correct; with 15 HCP and Kx support South accepts the invitational 3H to 4H, a sound game (26 HCP) - just runs past the partscore lesson.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C; North gives 2D preference and it makes +1 - routine preference wrinkle.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C shows the club suit; North raises clubs and South bids 3N (25 HCP) making - natural game beyond the partscore lesson.
Edge 6c-4d: open 1C (six-card suit) and rebid 2C, the correct edge choice; then a natural 3N (25 HCP) making +1 - sound, runs to game.
5c-4d: 1D then 2C; North responds 1NT then gives 2D preference, 2D +2 - responder-shape wrinkle.
5-5 minors: open 1D (higher), rebid 2C; North gives 2D preference and it makes +1 - clean lesson with a preference wrinkle.
5-5 minors: 1D open, 2C rebid clean, but 3H splinter continuation runs to 3NT past the teaching point.
Standard 5d4c mirror: open longest 1D, rebid 2C, partner prefers 2D; clean partscore.
Edge 6c4d: 6-card club opens 1C, rebid 2C; correct but edge family.
Edge 6c4d: open 1C, rebid 2C passed out; correct, near-duplicate of board 23's family.
5-5 minors: open higher 1D, rebid 2C, rests in partscore; shows the point exactly.
Core 5c4d point: open 1D (not 1C), rebid 2C; clean, sensible partscore.
Balanced 14, open 1C rebid 1NT is the point, but 1NT buries a biddable 4-card heart suit; 1H (up the line) equally natural.
Edge hand distorted: void in responder's spades + unshown 4-card heart suit; 25 combined HCP languishes in a 2S misfit.
Balanced: open 1D, rebid 1NT (forced, can't show hearts cheaply); auction then wanders through checkback to a 3S partscore.
5c4d + 4-card spade edge: South showed the major with 1S; rebidding the 5-card club suit (2C) is the equally-valid other branch.
Core 5c4d: open 1D, rebid 2C clean; stops in 2H underbidding a viable game (South stiff HK).
Core 5c4d: open 1D, rebid 2C correct; North under-passes 2C with 10 HCP, leaving 25 in a too-low partscore (not South's fault).
6-5: open the 5-card major 1S, rebid the 6-card minor 2C; exact 6-5 rule, sensible 2S partscore.
5d4c mirror: open 1D, rebid 2C, then a natural 3D invite with 15; clean.
5d4c minimum (11): open 1D, rebid 2C; South correctly declines the 2NT invite (normal spot though it fails on this layout).
Core 5c4d: open 1D, rebid 2C, partner prefers 2D; clean minimum partscore.
5d4c mirror: open 1D, rebid 2C, partner rebids 6-card spades to 2S; clean partscore.
Balanced 1C/1NT-rebid point is clean, but the auction overreaches to a failing 5C on only 25 HCP; silly game contract obscures the lesson.
Edge hand distorted (near-duplicate of 28): void in responder's spades + unshown 4-card hearts; 25 HCP stuck in a 2S misfit.
Core 5c4d minimum (11): open 1D, rebid 2C; South correctly passes partner's 3S invite; sensible partscore.
5C-4D: 1D buys the cheap 2C rebid; N takes preference to 2D. Point shown cleanly.
6C-4D: open longest 1C, natural 2C rebid over 1S; partner passes. Clean partscore.
Std 5D-4C: 1D/2C textbook, but auction climbs to 3NT which buries the open-suit point.
Std 5D-4C 1D/2C sound; 3NT on 25 HCP goes down 1 - contract a wrinkle for a beginner.
Balanced 14: 1D then 1NT rebid is the moment, but auction runs on to 3H past the point.
6C-4D open 1C correct, but partner's 1D and S's 2D raise turn it into a diamond fit; club length never the story.
1D/2C fine, but 4NT Blackwood to 5D on 26 combined HCP is far past a beginner open-suit lesson.
5C-4D: 1D buys the 2C rebid over 1S; N preferences to 2D. Clean minor partscore.
Std 5D-4C mirror: open longest 1D, natural 2C rebid, N to 2D. Model example.
5C-4D: 1D then cheap 2C over 1S; N to 2D. Point shown cleanly, partscore made.
5-5 minors: open higher 1D, rebid lower 2C is textbook; but auction ends in a failing 3NT down 1.
5-5 minors: open 1D, rebid 2C; partner passes clubs. Clean fit-finding partscore.
Std 5D-4C: open longest 1D, natural 2C rebid over 1H, N to 2D. Model example.
1D/2C fine, but S's 2H raising into 874 xxx after N's 2D lands in a failing 2H down 1; should pass 2D.
5C-4D: 1D buys the 2C rebid over 1S; N to 2D. Clean minor partscore, point shown.
Balanced 14: open 1D, rebid 1NT over 1S; N passes. Textbook minor-then-1NT.
Balanced 12, 4C-3D: open longer minor 1C, rebid 1NT over 1S; N passes. Clean.
5C-4D: 1D/2C correct; weak D974 4-card and stiff HA opposite N's 5 hearts a slight wrinkle, N passes 2C.
5C-4D: 1D buys the cheap 2C rebid over 1H; N to 2D. Clean minor partscore.
6C-4D: open longest 1C, natural 2C rebid over 1H; N raises to 3C. Clean club fit.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C is the clean cheap rebid; 2D partscore on 24 combined is sound.
6c-5h: open the 5-card major 1H, rebid the long minor 2C - the exact exception the lesson teaches.
6d-5h: open 5-card major 1H, rebid long minor 2D; clean illustration of the exception.
Balanced 14: open 1C, rebid 1NT is textbook, but auction runs on to 3S/3NT past the teaching point.
Balanced 13: open longer minor 1C, rebid 1NT and pass; clean minor-then-1NT example.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; 2D partscore sensible, clean mirror example.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; North's 2NT on 24 combined is reasonable, down-1 is a play matter.
Balanced 13, 4 diamonds: open 1D, rebid 1NT and pass; clean balanced example.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; North's 2H rebid lands a sensible partscore.
6c-4d edge: open longest 1C, rebid 2C - sound, but illustrates the ordinary case not the lesson's exception.
6d-5h: open 5-card major 1H, rebid long minor 2D; clean exception illustration.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; North passes, clean partscore example.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; North's 4S jump is his 7-card suit, makes - South's open/rebid is textbook.
5c-4d: open 1D, rebid 2C; clean partscore, makes +2.
5-5 minors: open higher 1D, rebid 2C; 3NT on 26 combined is a sensible final contract.
Open 1H/rebid 2D correct, but 9-call Blackwood auction to a failing 5S on 25 combined buries the beginner teaching point.
5-5 minors: open higher 1D, rebid 2C; clean 2D partscore example.
Open 1C/rebid 2C fine, but 3NT overreach on 23 combined goes down - a failing silly contract clouds the example.
5-5 minors, 15 HCP: open higher 1D, rebid 2C (not a reverse); clean 2C partscore.
6c-4d edge: open longest 1C, rebid 2C - sound but the ordinary case; North's 3C raise makes +1.
4=0=4=5 opens 1D (avoids the no-rebid 1C trap), 1S, 4-4 spade fit, 4S=25 HCP making 5; clean single point.
1C/2C opening point is fine, but 3N on 24 HCP with a stiff heart is unsound and goes down 1 (result 8) - silly contract buries the point.
6c4d opens 1C, rebids 2C on solid AKQJxx, N drives 3N; 25 HCP makes +1. Opening point clean.
5-5 minors opens 1D/rebids 2C correctly, but 3N on 23 HCP with two doubletons fails (result 8, down 1) - contract too aggressive.
5-5 minors opens 1D, rebids 2C; N shows 6 spades, S raises with Kx doubleton to 3S then 4S makes 5. Minor wrinkle raising a rebid on a doubleton.
6c4d opens 1C, rebids 2C; N shows/signs off 6 spades, S passes 2S with the stiff. Clean partscore, +1.
6d5s opens the major 1S then 3D (natural new suit over the 2/1) to show the long minor; N 3N makes on 23 HCP. 6-5 shows the point well.
Opens 1D correctly on void-4-4-5, but then a 4NT keycard auction lands in 5D down 1 (result 10) on 24 HCP - Blackwood on a minimum, wrong contract, distorts the point.
4=0=4=5 opens 1D, shows spades then clubs; 24 HCP 3N makes exactly. Sound but multi-round auction adds noise to the single point.
6c5s opens the major 1S, rebids 2C; N gives Kx preference to 2S, makes exactly. 6-5 open-the-major point clear.
4=0=4=5 opens 1D then 1S correctly; but with 15 opposite a 2S raise passing is an underbid (2S makes 5, result 11). Point is clean, close-out timid.
6d5h opens the major 1H then 2D for the long minor; N with 7 spades jumps to 4S, 25 HCP makes. Textbook 6-5 open-the-major.
Void-4-4-5 opens 1D, rebids 2C; N shows hearts, S jumps to 4H on the 4-4 fit + shape, makes +1. Sound, opening point clean.
6c5s opens 1S/rebids 3C fine, but the auction reaches 3N on a total misfit and goes down 4 (result 5) - disastrous contract wrecks the teaching board.
4=0=4=5 opens 1D then 1S correctly; N's weak 1N rebid closes it out at 1N making exactly (24 HCP, no major fit). South's calls are right; final spot low.
6c5h opens the major 1H, rebids 2C; long fourth-suit auction lands in the 5-2 heart fit at 3H making exactly. Point sound but auction busy.
6d5s opens the major 1S then 3D (natural, forcing) for the long minor; N raises to 4S on the 5-3 fit, makes +1. 6-5 point clear.
6c5h open-1H point is fine, but a 3N-4C-4NT-5S-6C keycard slam auction to 6C buries a beginner opening lesson - too far past the point.
6d5h opens the major 1H then 2D for the long minor; N gives heart preference, 2H makes exactly on the 5-2 fit. Opening point clean.
Void-4-4-5 opens 1D then 2C correctly; N signs off in 6-card spades at 2S, S passes with the void, makes exactly. Point clean, sensible misfit stop.
North jump-shifts 3D; South rebids 3NT and never shows the 6-club suit, so the rebid-the-minor point is invisible.
Opens correct major; North's limit raise to 3H finds the fit, so the illustrative 2D minor-rebid never appears.
Model auction: 1H open, 2D long-minor rebid, partner preferences to 2H; contract makes exactly.
Model auction: 1S open, 2C long-minor rebid, North preferences to 2S; only overtricks are a wrinkle.
3NT on combined 22 HCP goes down one; failing contract, poor teaching board.
After the correct 2C rebid, South's 2NT with a 1-5-1-6 shape is distorted and 2NT fails (down one).
South passes North's forcing 3H jump-shift, never rebids the pattern, and 3H goes down two.
Full pattern shown: 1H open, 2C long-minor rebid, North jumps to 4H on the fit; game makes.
5c-4d minors: opening 1D to buy the 2C rebid is the lesson's rule, but 1C (longest) is the standard choice.
Model auction: 1H open, 2D long-minor rebid, North preferences to 2H; overtricks only.
Opens major then rebids the long minor (3C) inside a 2/1 GF auction; sound but a notch above the beginner core.
Pattern shown, but North's leap to 4S on combined 22 HCP goes down one; failing game.
After the correct 3D rebid, North's 3NT on combined 24 fails (down one); poor result board.
Open major, rebid long minor (3C), reach a making 3NT; sound but inside a 2/1 GF auction beyond beginner core.
Textbook 1H-2D-2H shape, but the 5-2 heart partscore goes down one on the card lie; not a model result.
Opens correct major; North's leap to 4H ends the auction before the illustrative minor rebid appears; game makes.
Open major, rebid long minor (3C), reach a making 4S; sound but inside a 2/1 GF auction beyond beginner core.
Core 1H-2C pattern shown, then the auction runs into Blackwood and stops in 5H; keycard is well beyond the beginner point.
